name: B-59 Hydroacoustic Shack
description: A closet-sized compartment aft of the conning tower, separated from the main passageway by a sound-dampening curtain of rubberized canvas. The Sonar Analyst works here alone, bracketed by a cathode-ray oscilloscope and a manual tracing table, wearing Bakelite headphones that press the ocean's noise directly into his skull.
atmosphericDetails: The green cathode-ray trace pulses with every external detonation — a bright, spiking line that fades back to baseline in the seconds between events.
The manual tracing chart is covered in pencil marks — bearing lines, range estimates, time stamps — the accumulated record of six hours of tracking.
The headphone cable is long enough to reach the curtain but no further; to communicate with the conning tower, Volkov must remove the headphones and shout through the canvas.
